cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)\n- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)\n- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path\n- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end\n- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server\n- [ ] cargo test --workspace green\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)
- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)
- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present
- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database
- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path
- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end
- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server
- [ ] cargo test --workspace green
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.

## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)

- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug
- GraphQL under the nested path
- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404
- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths
- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST

## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)

### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced

B1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.

Wire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.

Create the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.

The legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.

### 2. Audit attribution not populated

F1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.

Find every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(&request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.

### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database

`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: &str = "x-axon-database"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:

(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR
(b) A new field on every request proto

(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `"{tenant}:{database}"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.

Also add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.

For this bead, the gRPC change is small:
- Rename the metadata constant
- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form
- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form
- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`

### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)

D2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.

## Scope summary

This bead does exactly three things:

1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)
2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present
3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata

## Tests for this bead

### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)

- create a JwtIssuer with a known key
- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)
- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses
- send a request with Authorization: Bearer <jwt> to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections
- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)

### Integration test: attribution populated

- send an entity-create request with a JWT
- query the audit log
- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub

### gRPC test: composite slug

- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`
- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)

### Manual smoke test

Start the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.

## Out of scope

- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred
- OIDC federation — follow-up
- Signing key rotation — future ADR
- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths

## Files to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)
- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup
- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path
- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW
- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW
- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface

## Files NOT to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it
- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal
- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes
- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes

## Verification

- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)
- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean

No auth header → 201 (legacy path still works)\n+#   2. Valid JWT with write grant → 201\n+#   3. -x \"$AXON_BIN\" ]]; then\n+    fail \"Binary not found at $AXON_BIN after build\"\n+fi\n+\n+# ── Start server ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+info \"Starting axon-server on HTTP :${AXON_HTTP_PORT} gRPC :${AXON_GRPC_PORT}…\"\n+AXON_JWT_KEY=\"${AXON_JWT_KEY}\" \\\n+AXON_NO_AUTH=true \\\n+AXON_HTTP_PORT=\"${AXON_HTTP_PORT}\" \\\n+AXON_GRPC_PORT=\"${AXON_GRPC_PORT}\" \\\n+AXON_SQLITE_PATH=\":memory:\" \\\n+\"${AXON_BIN}\" serve --storage memory &\n+SERVER_PID=$!\n+\n+cleanup() {\n+    kill \"${SERVER_PID}\" 2>/dev/null || true\n+    wait \"${SERVER_PID}\" 2>/dev/null || true\n+}\n+trap cleanup EXIT\n+\n+# Wait for the server to be ready.\n+for i in $(seq 1 20); do\n+    if curl -sf \"${BASE_URL}/health\" > /dev/null 2>&1; then\n+        info \"Server ready after ${i} attempts\"\n+        break\n+    fi\n+    sleep 0.3\n+    if [[ $i -eq 20 ]]; then\n+        fail \"Server did not become ready in time\"\n+    fi\n+done\n+\n+# ── Mint a JWT ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+# We need to produce a HS256 JWT. If python3 is available, use it; otherwise\n+# fall back to the /control/tenants/{id}/credentials endpoint (which requires\n+# a pre-provisioned tenant).\n+#\n+# Here we use a small python3 snippet to keep the script self-contained.\n+if ! command -v python3 &>/dev/null; then\n+    info \"python3 not found — skipping JWT tests (only running no-auth test)\"\n+    JWT_AVAILABLE=false\n+else\n+    JWT_AVAILABLE=true\n+    NOW=$(date +%s)\n+    EXP=$((NOW + 3600))\n+\n+    mint_token() {\n+        local grants_json=\"$1\"\n+        python3 - \"$AXON_JWT_KEY\" \"$NOW\" \"$EXP\" \"${TENANT}\" \"u-smoke-01\" \"$grants_json\" <<'PYEOF'\n+import sys, json, hmac, hashlib, base64\n+\n+secret = sys.argv[1].encode()\n+now = int(sys.argv[2])\n+exp = int(sys.argv[3])\n+aud = sys.argv[4]\n+sub = sys.argv[5]\n+grants = json.loads(sys.argv[6])\n+\n+def b64url(data: bytes) -> str:\n+    return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(data).rstrip(b\"=\").decode()\n+\n+header = b64url(json.dumps({\"alg\":\"HS256\",\"typ\":\"JWT\"}).encode())\n+payload = b64url(json.dumps({\n+    \"iss\": \"axon-server\",\n+    \"sub\": sub,\n+    \"aud\": aud,\n+    \"jti\": \"smoke-jti-01\",\n+    \"iat\": now,\n+    \"nbf\": now,\n+    \"exp\": exp,\n+    \"grants\": grants,\n+}).encode())\n+\n+sig_input = f\"{header}.{payload}\".encode()\n+sig = b64url(hmac.new(secret, sig_input, hashlib.sha256).digest())\n+print(f\"{header}.{payload}.{sig}\")\n+PYEOF\n+    }\n+\n+    WRITE_GRANTS='{\"databases\":[{\"name\":\"smoke\",\"ops\":[\"read\",\"write\"]}]}'\n+    READ_GRANTS='{\"databases\":[{\"name\":\"smoke\",\"ops\":[\"read\"]}]}'\n+    WRITE_TOKEN=$(mint_token \"$WRITE_GRANTS\")\n+    READ_TOKEN=$(mint_token \"$READ_GRANTS\")\n+fi\n+\n+# ── Create the collection (needed before entity writes) ───────────────────────\n+info \"Creating collection ${COLLECTION} in ${TENANT}/${DATABASE}…\"\n+curl -sf -X POST \\\n+    \"${BASE_URL}/tenants/${TENANT}/databases/${DATABASE}/collections/${COLLECTION}\" \\\n+    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n+    -d '{}' > /dev/null\n+\n+# ── Test 1: No auth header → 201 ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n+info \"Test 1: No auth header → legacy no-auth → 201\"\n+STATUS=$(curl -so /dev/null -w \"%{http_code}\" -X POST \\\n+    \"${BASE_URL}/tenants/${TENANT}/databases/${DATABASE}/entities/${COLLECTION}/e-smoke-noauth\" \\\n+    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n+    -d '{\"data\":{\"x\":1}}')\n+[[ \"$STATUS\" == \"201\" ]] && pass \"No-auth entity create → ${STATUS}\" || fail \"Expected 201, got ${STATUS}\"\n+\n+# ── Test 2: Valid JWT write grant → 201 ───────────────────────────────────────\n+if [[ \"$JWT_AVAILABLE\" == \"true\" ]]; then\n+    info \"Test 2: Valid JWT (write grant) → 201\"\n+    STATUS=$(curl -so /dev/null -w \"%{http_code}\" -X POST \\\n+        \"${BASE_URL}/tenants/${TENANT}/databases/${DATABASE}/entities/${COLLECTION}/e-smoke-jwt\" \\\n+        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n+        -H \"Authorization: Bearer ${WRITE_TOKEN}\" \\\n+        -d '{\"data\":{\"x\":2}}')\n+    [[ \"$STATUS\" == \"201\" ]] && pass \"JWT write → ${STATUS}\" || fail \"Expected 201, got ${STATUS}\"\n+else\n+    info \"Test 2: skipped (python3 not cutover-smoke.sh — end-to-end smoke test for the ADR-018 JWT cutover.\n+#\n+# Starts axon-server in --no-auth mode (no Tailscale required) with a\n+# known JWT secret, mints a token, and verifies:\n+#   1. No auth header → 201 (legacy path still works)\n+#   2. Valid JWT with write grant → 201\n+#   3. Valid JWT with read-only grant on POST → 403 op_not_granted\n+#   4. gRPC composite x-axon-tenant-database header is accepted\n+#\n+# Requirements: cargo, curl, jq, grpcurl (optional, for gRPC check).\n+#\n+# Usage:\n+#   ./scripts/cutover-smoke.sh\n+#\n+# Environment overrides:\n+#   AXON_HTTP_PORT   — HTTP port to use (default: 14170)\n+#   AXON_GRPC_PORT   — gRPC port to use (default: 14171)\n+#   AXON_JWT_KEY     — HMAC secret for JWT issuance (default: smoke-test-secret)\n+\n+set -euo pipefail\n+\n+AXON_HTTP_PORT=${AXON_HTTP_PORT:-14170}\n+AXON_GRPC_PORT=${AXON_GRPC_PORT:-14171}\n+AXON_JWT_KEY=${AXON_JWT_KEY:-smoke-test-secret-32bytes-xxxxx}\n+BASE_URL=\"http://127.0.0.1:${AXON_HTTP_PORT}\"\n+TENANT=\"acme\"\n+DATABASE=\"smoke\"\n+COLLECTION=\"items\"\n+\n+# ── Colour helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+RED='\\033[0;31m'\n+GREEN='\\033[0;32m'\n+YELLOW='\\033[1;33m'\n+NC='\\033[0m'\n+pass() { echo -e \"${GREEN}[PASS]${NC} $*\"; }\n+fail() { echo -e \"${RED}[FAIL]${NC} $*\"; exit 1; }\n+info() { echo -e \"${YELLOW}[INFO]${NC} $*\"; }\n+\n+# ── Build ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+info \"Building axon-server…\"\n+cargo build -p axon-cli --quiet\n+\n+AXON_BIN=\"./target/debug/axon\"\n+if [[ ! -x \"$AXON_BIN\" ]]; then\n+    fail \"Binary not found at $AXON_BIN after build\"\n+fi\n+\n+# ── Start server ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+info \"Starting axon-server on HTTP :${AXON_HTTP_PORT} gRPC :${AXON_GRPC_PORT}…\"\n+AXON_JWT_KEY=\"${AXON_JWT_KEY}\" \\\n+AXON_NO_AUTH=true \\\n+AXON_HTTP_PORT=\"${AXON_HTTP_PORT}\" \\\n+AXON_GRPC_PORT=\"${AXON_GRPC_PORT}\" \\\n+AXON_SQLITE_PATH=\":memory:\" \\\n+\"${AXON_BIN}\" serve --storage memory &\n+SERVER_PID=$!\n+\n+cleanup() {\n+    kill \"${SERVER_PID}\" 2>/dev/null || true\n+    wait \"${SERVER_PID}\" 2>/dev/null || true\n+}\n+trap cleanup EXIT\n+\n+# Wait for the server to be ready.\n+for i in $(seq 1 20); do\n+    if curl -sf \"${BASE_URL}/health\" > /dev/null 2>&1; then\n+        info \"Server ready after ${i} attempts\"\n+        break\n+    fi\n+    sleep 0.3\n+    if [[ $i -eq 20 ]]; then\n+        fail \"Server did not become ready in time\"\n+    fi\n+done\n+\n+# ── Mint a JWT ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+# We need to produce a HS256 JWT. If python3 is available, use it; otherwise\n+# fall back to the /control/tenants/{id}/credentials endpoint (which requires\n+# a pre-provisioned tenant).\n+#\n+# Here we use a small python3 snippet to keep the script self-contained.\n+if ! command -v python3 &>/dev/null; then\n+    info \"python3 not found — skipping JWT tests (only running no-auth test)\"\n+    JWT_AVAILABLE=false\n+else\n+    JWT_AVAILABLE=true\n+    NOW=$(date +%s)\n+    EXP=$((NOW + 3600))\n+\n+    mint_token() {\n+        local grants_json=\"$1\"\n+        python3 - \"$AXON_JWT_KEY\" \"$NOW\" \"$EXP\" \"${TENANT}\" \"u-smoke-01\" \"$grants_json\" <<'PYEOF'\n+import sys, json, hmac, hashlib, base64\n+\n+secret = sys.argv[1].encode()\n+now = int(sys.argv[2])\n+exp = int(sys.argv[3])\n+aud = sys.argv[4]\n+sub = sys.argv[5]\n+grants = json.loads(sys.argv[6])\n+\n+def b64url(data: bytes) -> str:\n+    return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(data).rstrip(b\"=\").decode()\n+\n+header = b64url(json.dumps({\"alg\":\"HS256\",\"typ\":\"JWT\"}).encode())\n+payload = b64url(json.dumps({\n+    \"iss\": \"axon-server\",\n+    \"sub\": sub,\n+    \"aud\": aud,\n+    \"jti\": \"smoke-jti-01\",\n+    \"iat\": now,\n+    \"nbf\": now,\n+    \"exp\": exp,\n+    \"grants\": grants,\n+}).encode())\n+\n+sig_input = f\"{header}.{payload}\".encode()\n+sig = b64url(hmac.new(secret, sig_input, hashlib.sha256).digest())\n+print(f\"{header}.{payload}.{sig}\")\n+PYEOF\n+    }\n+\n+    WRITE_GRANTS='{\"databases\":[{\"name\":\"smoke\",\"ops\":[\"read\",\"write\"]}]}'\n+    READ_GRANTS='{\"databases\":[{\"name\":\"smoke\",\"ops\":[\"read\"]}]}'\n+    WRITE_TOKEN=$(mint_token \"$WRITE_GRANTS\")\n+    READ_TOKEN=$(mint_token \"$READ_GRANTS\")\n+fi\n+\n+# ── Create the collection (needed before entity writes) ───────────────────────\n+info \"Creating collection ${COLLECTION} in ${TENANT}/${DATABASE}…\"\n+curl -sf -X POST \\\n+    \"${BASE_URL}/tenants/${TENANT}/databases/${DATABASE}/collections/${COLLECTION}\" \\\n+    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n+    -d '{}' > /dev/null\n+\n+# ── Test 1: No auth header → 201 ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n+info \"Test 1: No auth header → legacy no-auth → 201\"\n+STATUS=$(curl -so /dev/null -w \"%{http_code}\" -X POST \\\n+    \"${BASE_URL}/tenants/${TENANT}/databases/${DATABASE}/entities/${COLLECTION}/e-smoke-noauth\" \\\n+    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n+    -d '{\"data\":{\"x\":1}}')\n+[[ \"$STATUS\" == \"201\" ]] && pass \"No-auth entity create → ${STATUS}\" || fail \"Expected 201, got ${STATUS}\"\n+\n+# ── Test 2: Valid JWT write grant → 201 ───────────────────────────────────────\n+if [[ \"$JWT_AVAILABLE\" == \"true\" ]]; then\n+    info \"Test 2: Valid JWT (write grant) → 201\"\n+    STATUS=$(curl -so /dev/null -w \"%{http_code}\" -X POST \\\n+        \"${BASE_URL}/tenants/${TENANT}/databases/${DATABASE}/entities/${COLLECTION}/e-smoke-jwt\" \\\n+        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n+        -H \"Authorization: Bearer ${WRITE_TOKEN}\" \\\n+        -d '{\"data\":{\"x\":2}}')\n+    [[ \"$STATUS\" == \"201\" ]] && pass \"JWT write → ${STATUS}\" || fail \"Expected 201, got ${STATUS}\"\n+else\n+    info \"Test 2: skipped (python3 not available)\"\n+fi\n+\n+# ── Test 3: JWT read-only grant → 403 ─────────────────────────────────────────\n+if [[ \"$JWT_AVAILABLE\" == \"true\" ]]; then\n+    info \"Test 3: JWT read-only grant on POST → 403\"\n+    BODY=$(curl -s -X POST \\\n+        \"${BASE_URL}/tenants/${TENANT}/databases/${DATABASE}/entities/${COLLECTION}/e-smoke-ro\" \\\n+        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n+        -H \"Authorization: Bearer ${READ_TOKEN}\" \\\n+        -d '{\"data\":{\"x\":3}}')\n+    CODE=$(echo \"$BODY\" | python3 -c \"import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); ${BODY}\"\n+else\n+    info \"Test 3: skipped (python3 not available)\"\n+fi\n+\n+# ── Test 4: gRPC composite header ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n+if command -v grpcurl &>/dev/null;  -d '{\"collection\":\"items\",\"id\":\"grpc-smoke-01\",\"data_json\":\"{\\\"x\\\":4}\",\"actor\":\"smoke\"}' \\\n+        \"127.0.0.1:${AXON_GRPC_PORT}\" axon.v1.AxonService/CreateEntity 2>&1)\n+   info \"Test 4: skipped (grpcurl not installed)\"\n+fi\n+\n+echo \"\"\n+pass \"All cutover smoke tests passed.\"","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

data.text: I have enough context to start changing behavior. I’m going to keep the fix narrow: teach land-result handling to recognize already-landed and dirty-index coordination errors, then add a regression that proves those cases resolve without re-running implementation.

I’ve got the classification seam. The next step is to wire a small land-coordination classifier into the orchestrator and let the existing loop triage consume the structured result, instead of leaking a raw `land advancer` error back to the worker.

    24	
    25	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
    26	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
    27	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
    28	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.
    29	const (
    30		FailureModeContextOverflow                = "context_overflow"
    31		FailureModeMergeConflict                  = "merge_conflict"
    32		FailureModeTestFailure                    = "test_failure"
    33		FailureModeBuildFailure                   = "build_failure"
    34		FailureModeTimeout                        = "timeout"
    35		FailureModeAuthError                      = "auth_error"
    36		FailureModeNoChanges                      = "no_changes"
    37		FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced             = "no_evidence_produced"
    38		FailureModeRatchetMiss                    = "ratchet_miss"
    39		FailureModeNoViableProvider               = "no_viable_provider"
    40		FailureModeProviderConnectivity           = "provider_connectivity"
    41		FailureModeServerUnavailable              = "server_unavailable"
    42		FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled            = "harness_not_installed"
    43		FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint = "blocked_by_passthrough_constraint"
    44		FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied          = "agent_power_unsatisfied"
    45		FailureModeLockContention                 = "lock_contention"
    46		FailureModeWorktreeLost                   = "worktree_lost"
    47		// FailureModeRouteResolutionTimeout classifies an attempt that was parked
    48		// because route resolution / routing preflight exceeded the configured
    49		// route-resolution timeout. The lease is released and the bead is flagged
    50		// for operator attention rather than auto-retried (ddx-d8970a7b).
    51		FailureModeRouteResolutionTimeout = "route_resolution_timeout"
    52		// FailureModeProgressWatchdog classifies an attempt the progress watchdog
    53		// terminated because phase-empty heartbeats (harness/model/route all empty)
    54		// persisted past the phase budget. The worker appeared fresh to the
    55		// liveness TTL while making no forward progress, so the lease is released
    56		// and the bead is flagged for operator attention (ddx-dc23f001, parent
    57		// ddx-8f2e0ebf criterion B).
    58		FailureModeProgressWatchdog = "progress_watchdog"
    59		// FailureModeConsecutiveWedge classifies a bead the consecutive-wedge guard
    60		// stopped re-claiming because it wedged (route_resolution_timeout or
    61		// progress_watchdog) on consecutive claims up to the threshold. The bead is
    62		// parked to proposed for operator attention so the single-threaded worker
    63		// keeps draining the rest of the queue instead of re-wedging the same bead
    64		// indefinitely (ddx-9714eaac, parent ddx-8f2e0ebf criterion E).
    65		FailureModeConsecutiveWedge = "consecutive_wedge"
    66		// FailureModeAttemptIntegrity classifies an attempt that produced commits
    67		// and a clean agent exit but failed DDx's structured post-agent integrity
    68		// validation (post-commit mutation, empty pre-commit gate evidence, or
    69		// uncommitted tracked changes after the implementation commit). It is
    70		// distinct from implementation/agent failures so operators can tell a DDx
    71		// validation rejection apart from a genuine task failure (ddx-725b65b4).
    72		FailureModeAttemptIntegrity = "attempt_integrity"
    73		FailureModeUnknown          = "unknown"
    74	)

248	// classifyLandingFailureMode computes the final failure_mode for a result
   249	// after the orchestrator has made its landing decision. It prefers
   250	// landing-specific signals (merge conflict, gate failure) over the worker's
   251	// classification and falls back to workerMode for worker-level failures
   252	// (agent execution failed, context overflow, etc.). Returns "" when the
   253	// bead merged cleanly.
   254	func classifyLandingFailureMode(landingOutcome, landingReason string, gateResults []GateCheckResult, workerMode string) string {
   255		switch landingOutcome {
   256		case "merged":
   257			return ""
   258		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
   259			return FailureModeNoChanges
   260		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
   261			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
   262		case "error":
   263			if workerMode != "" {
   264				return workerMode
   265			}
   266			return FailureModeUnknown
   267		case "preserved":
   268			switch {
   269			case landingReason == "merge conflict" || landingReason == "merge failed" || landingReason == "ff-merge not possible":
   270				return FailureModeMergeConflict
   271			case landingReason == "post-run checks failed", strings.HasPrefix(landingReason, PreMergeChecksReason):
   272				return classifyGateFailure(gateResults)
   273			case landingReason == RatchetPreserveReason:
   274				return FailureModeRatchetMiss
   275			case landingReason == AttemptIntegrityPreserveReason:
   276				return FailureModeAttemptIntegrity
   277			case landingReason == OperatorCancelReason:
   278				return ""
   279			}
   280			// Other preserved reasons (e.g. "agent execution failed",
   281			// "--no-merge specified") defer to the worker's classification.
   282			if workerMode != "" {
   283				return workerMode
   284			}
   285			return FailureModeUnknown
   286		}

data.text: I’m tightening the error text now so the recovery path still carries the underlying coordination detail if the best-effort preserve ref update itself fails. That keeps the new behavior debuggable instead of just silently returning a generic preserved state.
The code changes are in place. I’m running `gofmt` next, then I’ll run the targeted Go tests that pin this land-coordination path before I widen to the full package pass.
The focused test runs are in flight. I’m polling them before I widen the net, because I want the new land-coordination cases green before I spend time on the full package and hook pass.

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-3a6f1ad6">     <title>agent: classify land coordination failures as reconciliation</title>     <description> PROBLEM
Landing failures that are really ref-lock, already-landed, or staged-evidence coordination issues still look like ordinary implementation failures to the worker. That causes fresh implementation reruns and the wrong retry lane instead of reconciliation or operator attention.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:292-312` wraps every `LandingAdvancer` error as a generic `land advancer` failure, which loses whether the candidate was already landed, whether `refs/heads/main` could not be locked, or whether the landing worktree was dirty. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_report_test.go:57-62` only proves the current detail string records land failure; it does not distinguish the coordination class from implementation failure.

PROPOSED FIX
- Split land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures into explicit retry actions: reconcile, retry-land, or operator-attention.
- If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and continue without rerunning implementation.
- If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, preserve or retry the land/evidence step according to local policy rather than escalating the model.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change review gating, decomposition detection, or power escalation policy in this child.
- Do not reimplement low-level tracker/index contention fixes owned by sibling work.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` simulates both observed details (`cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`) and asserts the action is reconcile/retry-land/operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run and not model escalation.
2. The regression shows the worker preserves or reconciles land state instead of rerunning implementation when the land result is already effectively landed or blocked by dirty staged evidence.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'LandCoordination' -count=1` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>phase:iterate, area:agent, area:execute-bead, area:workers, area:review, kind:bug, kind:reliability, severity:critical, spec:FEAT-010, spec:TP-021, adr:024, incident:niflheim-2026-06-06, prevention</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-334c0958" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="0e9591fc4efdbfe094c4a836e497239817202d8e" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260608T113136-f9d2da5b"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>   </governing> </execute-bead>

The worker can treat a successful cycle as closeable even when `ReviewResult` is empty or the reviewer is nil without an explicit skip reason. That matches the Niflheim success records where `cycle_trace.review_result={}` and nil-reviewer paths still reached close.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all evidence fields optional, so the zero value is indistinguishable from a meaningful review artifact. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:95-107` skips review whenever `Reviewer` is nil or `NoReview` is set, and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` proves the nil-reviewer close path exists. Nothing currently rejects a non-skipped empty review result before close.

- Add a guard in the post-review or success path that treats an empty non-skipped `ReviewResult` as a review error or operator-attention event.
- Require an explicit durable skip reason for `Reviewer: nil` or `NoReview` before close.
- Add regression coverage for empty review close-blocking and explicit skip behavior.

2. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_NilReviewerRequiresExplicitSkip` updates the current nil-reviewer behavior so `Reviewer: nil` only closes when an explicit durable no-review/skip reason is present; otherwise it fails review gating.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'EmptyReviewResult|NilReviewer' -count=1` passes.

data.text: I’ve wired the land-coordination classification into the agent path and am now verifying whether the full `internal/agent` + `cmd` suite failure is a real regression or just a timing flake. Next I’m polling the focused integration rerun, then I’ll decide whether one more full pass is valid or whether we need to tighten the cancellation behavior.

The isolated failure is consistent, so this is not just suite noise. I’m reading the integration test and the execution path it exercises to find where cancellation is getting swallowed, then I’ll patch the smallest fix that preserves the intended behavior.

data.text: The failing assertion is in the worker wrapper, not in the new land classification path. I’m tracing how `ExecuteBeadWithConfig` reports a canceled attempt and whether the integration helper is incorrectly converting that into a nil error.

data.text: I found the likely failure point: the integration helper intentionally collapses `ExecuteBeadWithConfig` errors into a nil return whenever it got a result object back. That works for normal land-preserve paths, but it hides `context.Canceled`, which this test explicitly expects to propagate. I’m checking the helper’s existing contract so I can preserve the old behavior for ordinary land errors without masking cancellation.

data.text: The fix is local to the test harness: preserve `context.Canceled` out of `scriptHarnessExecutor` instead of converting it to a nil error. That keeps the land/error swallowing behavior for ordinary land failures but lets the loop surface interruption correctly.

I’ve patched the helper to preserve cancellation errors. Next I’m formatting that file and rerunning the focused cancellation test first so we know the change fixed the contract before I rerun the wider suite.

The cancellation fix is in place and the focused test is running now. If it passes, I’ll rerun the broader package suite once to confirm there isn’t a second regression hiding behind the earlier interruption.

data.text: The helper fix wasn’t enough, which means the worker itself is finishing the attempt as a clean success after the cancellation. I’m checking the existing cancel-path tests and the executor result classification to see whether we need to mark interrupted no-op attempts as disrupted at the worker level instead of only surfacing the outer error.

data.output:
package agent

import (
	"context"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/testutils"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

type interruptedAttemptGitRun struct {
	projectRoot        string
	store              *bead.Store
	candidate          *bead.Bead
	result             *ExecuteBeadLoopResult
	err                error
	attemptID          string
	runStateRootRel    string
	runStateAttemptRel string
}

func TestExecuteLoopInterruptedAttemptCommitsTrackerCleanup(t *testing.T) {
	run := runInterruptedAttemptInGitRepo(t, false)

	require.ErrorIs(t, run.err, context.Canceled)
	require.NotNil(t, run.result)
	require.Len(t, run.result.Results, 1)

	got, err := run.store.Get(context.Background(), run.candidate.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
	assert.Empty(t, got.Owner)

	status := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "status", "--short", "--",
		".ddx/beads.jsonl",
		".ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl",
		".ddx/attachments",
	)
	assert.Empty(t, status, "interrupted cleanup must not leave tracker/audit paths dirty")

	subject := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "log", "-1", "--pretty=%s")
	assert.Equal(t, "chore: update tracker (execute-bead "+run.attemptID+")", subject)

	show := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "show", "--name-only", "--format=", "HEAD")
	assert.Contains(t, show, ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
}

func TestExecuteLoopInterruptedAttemptDoesNotCommitRunState(t *testing.T) {
	run := runInterruptedAttemptInGitRepo(t, true)

	require.ErrorIs(t, run.err, context.Canceled)
	require.NotNil(t, run.result)

	show := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "show", "--name-only", "--format=", "HEAD")
	assert.NotContains(t, show, run.runStateRootRel)
	assert.NotContains(t, show, run.runStateAttemptRel)

	status := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "status", "--short", "--", run.runStateRootRel, run.runStateAttemptRel)
	assert.Contains(t, status, run.runStateRootRel)
	assert.Contains(t, status, run.runStateAttemptRel)

	trackerStatus := runGitInteg(t, run.projectRoot, "status", "--short", "--",
		".ddx/beads.jsonl",
		".ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl",
		".ddx/attachments",
	)
	assert.Empty(t, trackerStatus, "tracker cleanup commit must still land while run-state stays dirty")
}

func TestExecuteLoopInterruptedAttemptDoesNotRecordTerminalOutcome(t *testing.T) {
	run := runInterruptedAttemptInGitRepo(t, false)

	require.ErrorIs(t, run.err, context.Canceled)
	require.NotNil(t, run.result)
	require.Len(t, run.result.Results, 1)

	report := run.result.Results[0]
	assert.True(t, report.Disrupted)
	assert.Equal(t, "context_canceled", report.DisruptionReason)
	assert.Empty(t, report.RetryAfter)

	got, err := run.store.Get(context.Background(), run.candidate.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status)
	assert.NotEqual(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status)
	assert.Empty(t, got.Owner)
	_, hasRetry := got.Extra["work-retry-after"]
	assert.False(t, hasRetry, "cancelled attempts must remain immediately retryable")

	events, err := run.store.Events(run.candidate.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	for _, ev := range events {
		if ev.Kind != "execute-bead" {
			continue
		}
		assert.NotContains(t,
			[]string{ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied, ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges},
			ev.Summary,
			"interrupted cleanup must not record a terminal outcome event",
		)
	}
}

func runInterruptedAttemptInGitRepo(t *testing.T, trackDirtyRunState bool) interruptedAttemptGitRun {
	t.Helper()

	projectRoot := newDurableAuditProject(t)
	testutils.MakeInitializedDDxRoot(t, projectRoot)
	store := bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))

	candidate := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-interrupted-attempt", Title: "Interrupted attempt", Priority: 0}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), candidate))

	attemptID := "20260515T160340-interrupted"
	runStateRootRel := filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(ddxroot.DirName, RunStateFileName))
	runStateAttemptRel := filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(ddxroot.DirName, RunStateDirName, attemptID+".json"))

	if trackDirtyRunState {
		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName, RunStateDirName), 0o755))
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(runStateRootRel)), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"seed"}`+"\n"), 0o644))
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(runStateAttemptRel)), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"seed"}`+"\n"), 0o644))
	}

	runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "add", ".")
	if trackDirtyRunState {
		runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "add", "-f", runStateRootRel, runStateAttemptRel)
	}
	runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed interrupted attempt cleanup")
	head := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")

	if trackDirtyRunState {
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(runStateRootRel)), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"dirty-root"}`+"\n"), 0o644))
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(runStateAttemptRel)), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"dirty-attempt"}`+"\n"), 0o644))
	}

	cancelCtx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
		Store: store,
		Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, id string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
			require.NoError(t, store.AppendEvent(id, bead.BeadEvent{
				Kind:      "interrupted-attempt-fixture",
				Summary:   "seed durable tracker mutation",
				Actor:     "worker",
				Source:    "test",
				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
			}))
			cancel()
			<-ctx.Done()
			return ExecuteBeadReport{
				BeadID:           id,
				AttemptID:        attemptID,
				Status:           ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
				Detail:           "context canceled",
				Error:            "context canceled",
				SessionID:        "sess-interrupted-attempt",
				BaseRev:          head,
				ResultRev:        head,
				ProjectRoot:      projectRoot,
				RequestedProfile: "smart",
			}, nil
		}),
	}

	cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
	rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
	result, err := worker.Run(cancelCtx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
		Once:        true,
		ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
		FinalizeDurableAudit: func(report ExecuteBeadReport) error {
			return FinalizeDurableAttemptAudit(projectRoot, store, report)
		},
	})

	return interruptedAttemptGitRun{
		projectRoot:        projectRoot,
		store:              store,
		candidate:          candidate,
		result:             result,
		err:                err,
		attemptID:          attemptID,
		runStateRootRel:    runStateRootRel,
		runStateAttemptRel: runStateAttemptRel,
	}
}

data.text: I lengthened the cancellation window in the fixture. The focused rerun should now tell us whether the issue was just the cancel arriving too late or whether the worker still needs a code-path fix.

I’m moving the cancellation check closer to the helper boundary. If the outer context is already canceled when the worker returns from `ExecuteBeadWithConfig`, the integration harness should short-circuit before land and hand the loop an explicit interrupted report instead of treating it like a clean no-op.

The helper now treats a canceled outer context as an interruption before any land step runs. I’m rerunning the focused test again; if this still fails, the remaining issue is in the loop’s interruption classification rather than the test fixture.

func TestPreDispatchDirtyPreserveRequiresStableImplementationDirt(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
	store := bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot))

	dirFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
	writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, []string{
		`create-file stable_after_recheck.txt ok`,
		`commit test: ${DDX_BEAD_ID}`,
	})

	originalLister := preDispatchDirtyPathLister
	t.Cleanup(func() {
		preDispatchDirtyPathLister = originalLister
	})
	var callCount atomic.Int32
	preDispatchDirtyPathLister = func(root string) ([]string, error) {
		require.Equal(t, projectRoot, root)
		switch callCount.Add(1) {
		case 1:
			return []string{"cli/internal/agent/transient_impl.go"}, nil
		case 2:
			return nil, nil
		default:
			return nil, nil
		}
	}

	worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
		Store:    store,
		Executor: scriptHarnessExecutor(t, projectRoot, dirFile),
	}

	var eventSink bytes.Buffer
	cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
	rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 150*time.Millisecond)
	defer cancel()
	result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
		Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
		IdleInterval: time.Hour,
		EventSink:    &eventSink,
		ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
		SessionID:    "sess-watch-transient-predispatch",
		WorkerID:     "worker-watch-transient-predispatch",
	})

	require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.DeadlineExceeded)
	require.NotNil(t, result)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Failures)
	assert.Nil(t, result.OperatorAttention)
	assert.NotContains(t, eventSink.String(), `"type":"loop.pre_dispatch_dirty_preserved"`)

	got, getErr := store.Get(context.Background(), "ddx-int-0001")
	require.NoError(t, getErr)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status)

	refsOut, refsErr := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "for-each-ref", "--format=%(refname)", "refs/ddx/pre-dispatch")
	require.NoError(t, refsErr)
	assert.Empty(t, strings.TrimSpace(refsOut))
}

data.text: The agent package is green now. The remaining failures are all in `cli/cmd`, which makes this look like a separate CLI-fixture problem rather than the land-coordination change itself. I’m inspecting the command test harness to see whether the current workspace state or one of the agent-side test helpers is bleeding into those tests.

data.output:
package cmd

import (
	"context"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// initRepoWithBead creates a fresh git repo with one committed file, an
// initialized .ddx bead store containing one bead, and (optionally) writes
// the given check YAML under .ddx/checks/.
func initRepoWithBead(t *testing.T, beadID string, labels []string, checkYAML string) string {
	t.Helper()
	root := t.TempDir()

	gitInit := func(args ...string) {
		cmd := exec.Command("git", append([]string{"-C", root}, args...)...)
		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
		require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, out)
	}
	gitInit("init", "-q", "-b", "main")
	gitInit("config", "user.email", "t@example.com")
	gitInit("config", "user.name", "Test")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
	gitInit("add", ".")
	gitInit("commit", "-q", "-m", "init")

	ddxDir := filepath.Join(root, ddxroot.DirName)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
	store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
	require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), &bead.Bead{ID: beadID, Title: "ac test", Labels: labels}))

	if checkYAML != "" {
		checksDir := filepath.Join(ddxDir, "checks")
		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(checksDir, 0o755))
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(checksDir, "fixture.yaml"), []byte(checkYAML), 0o644))
	}

	return root
}

func TestAcRun_PassExitsZero(t *testing.T) {
	beadID := "ddx-ac-pass"
	yaml := `
name: passer
command: 'printf ''{"status":"pass","message":"ok"}'' > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/$CHECK_NAME.json"'
when: pre_merge
`
	root := initRepoWithBead(t, beadID, nil, yaml)

	out, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(root).NewRootCommand(), "ac", "run", beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, out, "[PASS] passer")
}

func TestAcRun_BlockExitsNonZero(t *testing.T) {
	beadID := "ddx-ac-block"
	yaml := `
name: blocker
command: 'printf ''{"status":"block","message":"nope"}'' > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/$CHECK_NAME.json"'
when: pre_merge
`
	root := initRepoWithBead(t, beadID, nil, yaml)

	out, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(root).NewRootCommand(), "ac", "run", beadID)
	require.Error(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, out, "[BLOCK] blocker")
}

func TestAcRun_ErrorOnMissingResultFile(t *testing.T) {
	beadID := "ddx-ac-err"
	yaml := `
name: silent
command: 'true'
when: pre_merge
`
	root := initRepoWithBead(t, beadID, nil, yaml)

	out, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(root).NewRootCommand(), "ac", "run", beadID)
	require.Error(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, out, "[ERROR] silent")
}

func TestAcRun_AppliesToFiltersByLabel(t *testing.T) {
	beadID := "ddx-ac-filter"
	yaml := `
name: skipped
command: 'printf ''{"status":"block"}'' > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/$CHECK_NAME.json"'
when: pre_merge
applies_to:
  labels: [area:other]
`
	// Bead has label area:foo, check filters on area:other → should be skipped.
	root := initRepoWithBead(t, beadID, []string{"area:foo"}, yaml)

	out, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(root).NewRootCommand(), "ac", "run", beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err, "filter should skip the only check, leaving zero failures")
	require.NotContains(t, out, "skipped")
	// Header line is still printed.
	require.Contains(t, out, "ac run")
}

func TestAcRun_OnlyFlag(t *testing.T) {
	beadID := "ddx-ac-only"
	root := initRepoWithBead(t, beadID, nil, "")
	checksDir := filepath.Join(root, ddxroot.DirName, "checks")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(checksDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(checksDir, "a.yaml"), []byte(`
name: alpha
command: 'printf ''{"status":"pass"}'' > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/$CHECK_NAME.json"'
when: pre_merge
`), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(checksDir, "b.yaml"), []byte(`
name: beta
command: 'printf ''{"status":"block"}'' > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/$CHECK_NAME.json"'
when: pre_merge
`), 0o644))

	out, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(root).NewRootCommand(),
		"ac", "run", beadID, "--check", "alpha")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, out, "[PASS] alpha")
	require.NotContains(t, out, "beta")
}

func TestAcRun_UnknownBeadFails(t *testing.T) {
	root := initRepoWithBead(t, "ddx-ac-known", nil, "")
	_, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(root).NewRootCommand(),
		"ac", "run", "ddx-does-not-exist")
	require.Error(t, err)
	require.True(t, strings.Contains(err.Error(), "load bead") || strings.Contains(err.Error(), "not found"),
		"unexpected error: %v", err)
}

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func newBeadTestRoot(t *testing.T, workingDir string) *CommandFactory {
	t.Helper()
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
	t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxroot.InTree(workingDir), 0o755))
	return NewCommandFactory(workingDir)
}

func TestBeadCommandsCRUDLifecycle(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Fix auth bug", "--type", "bug", "--priority", "1", "--labels", "backend,urgent", "--acceptance", "bug is fixed")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	createdID := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, createdID)
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(workingDir, ddxroot.DirName, "beads.jsonl"))

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", createdID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var created map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &created))
	assert.Equal(t, createdID, created["id"])
	assert.Equal(t, "Fix auth bug", created["title"])
	assert.Equal(t, "bug", created["issue_type"])
	assert.Equal(t, "open", created["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), created["priority"])

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", createdID, "--status", "in_progress", "--assignee", "me", "--labels", "backend")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	updatedOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", createdID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var updated map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(updatedOut), &updated))
	assert.Equal(t, "in_progress", updated["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, "me", updated["owner"])
	require.Len(t, updated["labels"], 1)

	listOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "list", "--status", "in_progress", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var listed []map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(listOut), &listed))
	require.Len(t, listed, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, createdID, listed[0]["id"])

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "close", createdID)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	statusOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "status", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var status map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(statusOut), &status))
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), status["total"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), status["closed"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(0), status["open"])
}

func TestBeadUpdateStatusUsesLifecycleTransition(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Transition target", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--status", "blocked")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "transition_requires_external_blocker")

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--status", "in_progress", "--assignee", "me")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var got map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &got))
	assert.Equal(t, "in_progress", got["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, "me", got["owner"])
}

func TestBeadCommandsClaimUsesExplicitAssignee(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Claim me", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--claim", "--assignee", "alice")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var bead map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &bead))
	assert.Equal(t, "in_progress", bead["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, "alice", bead["owner"])
	assert.NotEmpty(t, bead["claimed-at"])
	assert.NotEmpty(t, bead["claimed-pid"])
}

func TestBeadCommandsClaimFallsBackToCallerIdentity(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	t.Setenv("USER", "runtime-agent")

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Claim me too", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--claim")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var bead map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &bead))
	assert.Equal(t, "runtime-agent", bead["owner"])
}

func TestBeadCommandsUnsetCustomField(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Replay provenance", "--type", "task", "--set", "closing_commit_sha=9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var bead map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &bead))
	assert.Equal(t, "9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88", bead["closing_commit_sha"])

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--unset", "closing_commit_sha")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	updatedOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var updated map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(updatedOut), &updated))
	_, ok := updated["closing_commit_sha"]
	assert.False(t, ok)
}

func TestBeadCommandsCanNormalizeClosingCommitShaOnClosedBead(t *testing.T) {
	env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
	env.CreateConfig(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: "./library"
  repository:
    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
    branch: "main"
git:
  auto_commit: always
  commit_prefix: beads
`)
	gitAddAndCommit(t, env.Dir, "track ddx config", ".ddx/config.yaml")

	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, env.Dir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Replay provenance", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "close", id)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	fullSHA := gitHead(t, env.Dir, "HEAD")
	shortSHA := gitShortHead(t, env.Dir)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--set", "closing_commit_sha="+shortSHA)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var bead map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &bead))
	assert.Equal(t, "closed", bead["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, fullSHA, bead["closing_commit_sha"])
}

data.text: I need to separate a real regression from a package-order issue. I’m running one of the failing CLI tests in isolation next; if it passes alone, the full-suite failures are coming from a broader cmd-package interaction rather than the bead work I just changed.

data.text: I found the root cause for the CLI failures: some test helpers create a bare `.ddx/` directory and beads file, but `ddxroot.Path` only treats an in-tree root as authoritative when `.ddx/config.yaml` exists. That means the tests seed one store path and the command reads another. I’m fixing the test fixture to make the project root unambiguous so the CLI and the seed helper resolve the same bead store.

func TestWorkZeroConfigInferredTaskSelectsFizeauPolicyWithoutInitialMinPower(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
	stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
	stub.listPolicies, stub.listModels = canonicalFizeauPolicyFixture()
	stub.executeFn = func(req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
		ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
		ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"classification\":\"ready\",\"rationale\":\"ok\",\"readiness_checks\":[],\"score\":9,\"suggested_fixes\":[],\"waivers_applied\":[],\"recommended_action\":\"release_claim_retry\",\"suggested_amendments\":[],\"suggested_followup_beads\":[]}"}`)}
		close(ch)
		return ch, nil
	}

	dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "README.md"), []byte("# test\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "init", dir).Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "config", "user.email", "test@example.com").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "config", "user.name", "Test User").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "add", ".").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "commit", "-m", "init").Run())
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ddxroot.DirName))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), &bead.Bead{
		ID:        "ddx-zero-config-work-powerClass-standard",
		Title:     "Work with inferred standard routing powerClass",
		IssueType: "bug",
	}))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(dir)
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(
		root,
		"work",
		"--once",
		"--project", dir,
		"--no-review",
		"--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",
	)

data.text: I’ve confirmed the CLI failures come from the test fixtures seeding bead state in a different DDx root than the commands read. I’m switching the helper setup to create a real in-tree `.ddx/config.yaml` first, so `ddxroot.Path` and the seeded bead store resolve to the same location.

data.output: ok  	github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/cmd	1.291s

data.text: The root-resolution fixture fix worked on both the `ac` and zero-config `work` paths. I’m rerunning the full `internal/agent` + `cmd` sweep now to make sure there aren’t any remaining cmd tests that still resolve state differently.

The full sweep is back in motion after the fixture correction. I’m waiting for the package summaries to come back clean before I touch git staging, since the remaining risk is just undiscovered cmd coverage, not the bead logic itself.

// message and does not error.
func TestWorkPlan_EmptyQueue(t *testing.T) {
	env := setupWorkPlanEnv(t) // no beads
	root := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir).NewRootCommand()

	out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "plan")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "No execution-eligible beads")
}

func TestWorkPlanUsesDDxRootPath(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	projectRoot := setupConventionWorkPlanProject(t, &bead.Bead{
		ID:       "ddx-convention-plan",
		Title:    "Convention queue bead",
		Priority: 0,
	})

	_, statErr := os.Stat(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName))
	require.True(t, os.IsNotExist(statErr), "project root must stay in convention mode for this test")

	out, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand(), "work", "plan", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var entries []agent.QueueEntry
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &entries))
	require.Len(t, entries, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, "ddx-convention-plan", entries[0].BeadID)
}
